message:
fix(http): deduplicate Connection headers when setting close/keep-alive

add_connection_close() and add_connection_keep_alive() previously
iterated headers and overwrote every Connection header's value. If a
response carried multiple Connection headers (legal per HTTP/1.1 but
uncommon), all of them would be rewritten, producing duplicate
"Connection: close" or "Connection: keep-alive" entries.

Both functions now use retain_mut: the first matching Connection header
is rewritten in place while subsequent duplicates are removed. This
guarantees exactly one Connection header in the finalized response
regardless of how many the upstream handler emitted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
